arc_function: Volume 1 traces the diplomatic breakdown from analytical framework through the August 1939 crisis to war declaration and the phony war paradox, establishing contingency arguments through documentary evidence and multi-perspective witnessing
dominant_engine: Documentary evidence accumulation with analytical counterfactual framework
state_progression: Analytical framework established for non-inevitability argument
Diplomatic crisis reaches turning point as Anglo-Polish treaty forces cancellation
War declared with paradoxical calm and minimal military engagement
Phony war documented through multi-national diary polyphony and official contradictions
Scholarly apparatus and primary directives appended as evidentiary payoff

turning_points.function: Last-moment cancellation of invasion due to unexpected diplomatic intervention; reveals fragility of aggressor's position and cascading effect of sequential diplomatic arrivals
War declaration paradox documented; official state of war contrasted with actual inactivity and private emotional responses across nations

setup_payoff_rhythm: Analytical thesis introduced early (ch.6) with promised evidentiary support; diplomatic crisis creates rising tension (ch.8); phony war aftermath documents consequences (ch.11); scholarly apparatus fulfills documentary promise (ch.12)
reusable_mechanisms: Counterfactual reasoning against determinism establishing contingency thesis
Thesis-first organization with evidence to follow pattern
Framing chapter via appended documents as evidentiary foundation
Decade-reflective temporal positioning establishing contemporary relevance
Sequential diplomatic arrivals creating cascading tension
Last-moment cancellation/rescinding of planned action at dramatic threshold
Divided alliance revealing partner self-interest over commitment
Treaty anatomy showing public terms versus secret protocols
Personal diary integration conveying private reactions and emotions
Contrast perspective displaying different moods across capitals simultaneously
Cascading information where each diplomatic message alters subsequent calculations
Multiple diary excerpts from different nations/classes creating emotional polyphony
Specific trivial detail grounding larger historical shifts
Contrast between official policy documents and private sentiment
Documentary evidence (telegrams, orders, directives) as narrative devices
Paradox construction: declared state with minimal actual engagement
Appended authentic primary documents as evidentiary payoff
Graduated response planning balancing multiple fronts
failure_modes: Non-fiction content lacks narrative protagonist for reader identification
Analytical apparatus interrupts story flow if not clearly framed
Multiple named historical figures increase cognitive load
Documentary density may fatigue readers expecting narrative conventions
Absence of combat action may underwhelm readers expecting war narrative
Scholarly citation may read as academic rather than engaging prose

craft_profile.pov_distance: Scholarly third-person omniscient with direct reader address; shifts to first-person through embedded diary excerpts; occasional diary reveals private emotional states
craft_profile.sentence_rhythm: Variable density mixing short declarative military orders with longer analytical sentences building atmospheric context
craft_profile.paragraphing: Extended continuous prose with thematic breaks; dense information paragraphs punctuated by single-sentence dramatic emphasis
craft_profile.dialogue_method: Reported speech reconstructed from historical records; occasional quoted phrases; minimal direct dialogue in favor of documented statements
craft_profile.description_method: Concrete specificity over abstraction—named entities, exact times, individual fates rather than categorical descriptions; brief atmospheric sketches rather than extended scene-setting
craft_profile.exposition_method: Thesis-statement-driven with scholarly context; information embedded in chronological narration rather than retrospective summary; citations serve as evidence trails
craft_profile.hook_delivery: Documentary appendices precede analytical text; chapters end with personal details or unresolved tensions rather than thematic closure; supplementary material catalogs document references
craft_profile.transition_method: Time stamps and location changes mark shifts between capitals and nations; bracket markers signal segment transitions from narrative to apparatus

risk_flags: Source is non-fiction historical analysis with no narrative fiction content
Most chapter cards (7,9,10) failed LLM parsing requiring fallback
Chapter 18 is supplementary placeholder material with minimal content
Schema designed for narrative fiction extraction with limited applicability
Confidence low due to non-applicable source type and parsing failures
